title: messages.rs: total_chunks×chunk_size (10 GiB) exceeds size_bytes cap (1 GiB)
description: FileHeader validation has an inconsistency between two independent caps: total_chunks is capped at 1024 and chunk data at 10 MiB each (enforced by deserialize_chunk_data), but size_bytes is capped at 1 GiB. A malicious peer can send FileHeader with size_bytes=1 and total_chunks=1024, then deliver 1024 chunks of 10 MiB each for a total of 10 GiB. The receiver's reassembly logic allocates based on chunks actually received, not the declared size_bytes. The size_bytes cap is therefore decorative for actual transfer-size enforcement. The mismatch allows a hostile MLS peer to deliver 10x more data than size_bytes implies is possible. Fix: either reduce total_chunks×MAX_CHUNK_DATA to match size_bytes max, or enforce that size_bytes <= total_chunks×chunk_size at deserialization time.
